title: BCM Stellar Wave Registry -- Scale-Invariance Test Dataset
author: Stephen Justin Burdick Sr. -- Emerald Entities LLC
description: Real published stellar parameters for BCM tachocline substrate wave solver. All values from peer-reviewed sources. No per-star tuning. Same wave equation as galactic and planetary solvers.
note: m_observed = azimuthal mode number inferred from dominant observable pattern. 0 = unknown/anomalous.

Sun.observable: 4-sector solar wind pattern; Hale 22-year magnetic cycle (m=2 magnetic, m=4 wind)
Sun.notes: Control star. Convection zone depth from helioseismology. 4-sector solar wind = substrate m=4 standing mode.

citations.B_tachocline: Charbonneau & MacGregor 1993 -- estimated from dynamo models
citations.sigma: Spitzer 1962 -- plasma conductivity at tachocline conditions
citations.v_conv: Miesch et al. 2008, ApJ 673 -- helioseismic inversions

Tabby.observable: Aperiodic flux dips 0.1-22% (Boyajian 2016). No clean periodic signal. Secular fading ~0.16 mag/century.
Tabby.notes: KIC 8462852. F-type stars have shallow convection zones. Rapid apparent rotation uncertain -- photometric period ~20d from Makarov & Goldin 2016. Anomalous dips unclassified. BCM prediction: substrate mode produces directed non-periodic flux modulation signature. If m_predicted != 0, substrate is active but aperiodic.

citations.no_aliens: Lisse et al. 2015 -- comet swarm hypothesis; Wright et al. 2016 -- KIC 8462852 as anomaly

Proxima.observable: Single active longitude. Superflare events. 83.5d rotation period. Proxima b habitable zone candidate.
Proxima.notes: Fully convective -- no tachocline. Substrate coupling degraded or absent at tachocline layer. Single active longitude = dipole m=1 mode from surface convection. BCM: without tachocline interface, substrate field cannot form standing modes above m=1. Analogous to galaxy with no SMBH -- no controller.

citations.fully_convective: Chabrier & Baraffe 1997 -- M < 0.35 Msun fully convective

EV_Lac.observable: Two active longitude flare clustering. Rapid rotator. High-amplitude flares. Strong field.
EV_Lac.notes: Near the fully-convective boundary. Rapid rotation and strong field suggest partial tachocline. Two active longitudes = m=2 quadrupole -- BCM predicts this as lowest stable mode above dipole when partial tachocline present. Analog: transition galaxy between Class I and V.

citations.two_active_long: Bidin et al. 2006 -- active longitude clustering in M flare stars
citations.mass_radius: Delfosse et al. 2000 -- M dwarf mass-luminosity

HR_1099.observable: Persistent two active longitude structure. RS CVn binary (K1IV + G5V). Tidally synchronized rotation.
HR_1099.notes: RS CVn binary system V711 Tau. Tidal forcing from binary companion = bar-channeling analog in BCM. Persistent m=2 active longitude structure maintained by tidal boundary condition -- not internal instability. Analogous to NGC3953 bar dipole. Substrate is being directed, not depleted.

citations.active_long: Berdyugina & Tuominen 1998, A&A 336 -- two active longitudes

Epsilon_Eri.observable: Active young solar analog. 11.68d rotation. Circumstellar disk detected. 3-sector wind structure inferred from magnetic mapping.
Epsilon_Eri.notes: Young active K dwarf. Faster rotation than Sun = stronger substrate coupling predicted. Confirmed debris disk. 3-sector magnetic structure from ZDI mapping. BCM: intermediate mode between solar m=4 and fully active m=2. Good test of rotation-rate dependence.

citations.B_mapping: Jeffers et al. 2014, A&A 569 -- ZDI magnetic maps
